message:
feat(fnx-algorithms): add Wilson's algorithm for uniform random spanning trees

Implements random_spanning_tree() and random_spanning_tree_directed() using
Wilson's loop-erased random walk algorithm. This generates spanning trees
uniformly at random in O(n * mean_hitting_time) expected time.

The implementation:
- For undirected graphs: picks arbitrary root, random walks from each
  unvisited node until hitting the growing tree, adds loop-erased path
- For directed graphs: generates random spanning arborescence rooted at
  a specified node using predecessor walks

Includes tests for:
- Triangle graph (basic connectivity)
- Disconnected graph detection
- Single node edge case
- Deterministic seeding
- Directed arborescence

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
